articles.title: Did Islam Makhachev Back Down From Ilia Topuria?
articles.description: Did Islam Makhachev turn down a fight with Ilia Topuria — again? Chael Sonnen dives into the growing debate surrounding the potential superfight between Islam Makhachev and Ilia Topuria. According to reports circulating in MMA media, Topuria accepted an offer to fight Islam — but the fight never materialized. Chael walks through the full timeline. One year ago, Topuria vacated the featherweight title and moved up to lightweight, aggressively campaigning for a fight with Makhachev. At the time, Khabib Nurmagomedov publicly pushed back, arguing that Topuria should earn a ranking at 155 before challenging the champion. Soon after, Islam himself vacated the lightweight title to move up to welterweight, preventing the matchup entirely. Now the story may have repeated itself. Topuria and his manager Malki Kawa claim that a fight with Islam was recently offered for the White House card and that Topuria immediately accepted. However, the fight reportedly fell apart — with explanations ranging from injury to financial negotiations. Chael explains why the situation raises an interesting question about legacy. If two dominant champions from the same era never meet in the cage, how will history decide who was truly the better fighter? And one key detail still hasn’t been answered: What weight class was the fight supposed to happen at? #UFC #IslamMakhachev #IliaTopuria #ChaelSonnen #Khabib #MMA 0:00 Did Islam back down from Ilia Topuria? 0:15 Chael explains the controversy 0:38 Topuria vacates featherweight title 1:00 Ilia campaigns for the Islam fight 1:26 Charles Oliveira pushes back 1:45 Khabib explains why Islam wouldn’t fight Ilia 2:12 Islam vacates the belt and moves to 170 2:45 Comparing Islam and Ilia’s careers 3:24 Could Ilia have been a triple champion? 4:00 Why Chael favors Ilia in pound-for-pound debate 4:26 Islam responds to Chael’s criticism 4:49 Ilia claims he accepted the fight 5:20 Justin Gaethje’s White House card story 5:59 Would Ilia fight Islam at 170? 6:30 The Conor McGregor comparison 7:00 The unanswered question: what weight class? articles.title: Chael Responds To White House Card Critics
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at takeultra.com! #UltraPouches #adBad Some fans and fighters are saying the UFC White House card “sucks.” Chael Sonnen completely disagrees. In this breakdown, Chael explains why the criticism surrounding the rumored White House event may be missing the bigger picture. According to Chael, fans often get caught up in fantasy matchmaking — imagining dream fights involving stars like Jon Jones, Conor McGregor, or Alex Pereira — without realizing how difficult it is to actually put those fights together. Chael points back to UFC 300, which many people initially criticized when the card was announced. But once the event unfolded, it delivered one of the greatest nights in UFC history, including the unforgettable Max Holloway vs Justin Gaethje finish and multiple dramatic moments that nobody predicted. That’s the key point. The UFC can only book the fighters who say yes. When the promotion puts together an event, they’re bringing the best matchups available at that moment. In the case of the White House card, Chael argues the promotion has already delivered three of the biggest stars in the sport — Sean O’Malley, Alex Pereira, and Ilia Topuria — plus Justin Gaethje, one of the most respected fighters in MMA. And once the fights actually happen, the real drama begins. articles.title: Francis Ngannou Beat the System...
articles.description: Would you rather be a world champion… or make millions of dollars? Chael Sonnen breaks down the incredible career arc of Francis Ngannou and why his story may be one of the most remarkable in combat sports history. Francis captured the UFC heavyweight championship, defeating Stipe Miocic to become the undisputed king of the division. But instead of staying put for the expected superfight with Jon Jones, Ngannou took a completely different path. Leaving the UFC, Francis bet on himself. Ngannou shocked the sports world when he secured a blockbuster boxing match against Tyson Fury—a fight most people believed would never happen. In one of the most surprising performances in combat sports, Ngannou dropped Fury and arguably did enough to win on the scorecards. The fight instantly turned him into a global attraction. From there, Ngannou landed another massive payday against Anthony Joshua, reportedly earning tens of millions of dollars. Add in his PFL deal, signing bonus, and guaranteed purses, and suddenly the narrative around his career looks very different. For years fighters have debated the ultimate question: Do you chase the belt, or do you chase the money? According to Chael, Francis Ngannou might be the rare fighter who did both. Watch the full breakdown as Chael explains why Ngannou’s gamble may have rewritten the playbook for fighters forever. #FrancisNgannou #UFC #ChaelSonnen #TysonFury #PFL #Boxing #MMA Francis Ngannou’s career story – 00:00 The champion vs money question – 00:15 Why younger fighters choose the belt – 00:28 Ngannou becomes UFC heavyweight champion – 00:51 The Jon Jones fight that never happened – 01:05 How Ngannou landed the Tyson Fury fight – 01:27 Why Chael calls Fury vs Ngannou historic – 01:48 Did Ngannou actually beat Tyson Fury? – 02:00 The Anthony Joshua payday – 02:20 Ngannou’s PFL deal explained – 02:35 Why promotions struggled working with Francis – 03:00 The revenue reality of big purses – 03:30 Jake Paul entering the Ngannou story – 04:05 Why Francis may have rewritten the playbook – 04:30 The final verdict on Ngannou’s gamble – 04:38 Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.description: Ronda Rousey said they originally had a great deal with Dana White for the Carano fight on December show but once the fight got pushed into 2026, the Paramount+ deal nixed it. "We were going to do a New Year's and it was going to be under the last fight under the pay -per -view model and he offered me the best pay -per -view structure ever and I was so grateful but then you know Gina said she needed more time to get in the best shape possible... And once they moved it to the streaming model it's just not about putting on the best fights possible. You know anymore." articles.title: The Truth About Jon Jones’ UFC Demands
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at takeultra.com! #UltraPouches #ad Jon Jones is not wrong — but Chael Sonnen says he may be misunderstanding the business. In this episode, Chael breaks down the growing debate around Jon Jones, UFC contracts, fighter pay, pay-per-view points, and Jones’ reported demands for a return fight. Chael makes it clear from the start: this is actually a defense of Jon Jones. He understands why Jones thinks the way he does, especially coming from the old Zuffa era, when the UFC often paid stars above and beyond their contracts for big main events, dangerous matchups, and short-notice saves. But the business has changed. Chael explains why a fighter’s true value, in the UFC’s eyes, starts with the contract already signed — not with outside comparisons to Jake Paul, Mike Tyson, Conor Benn, or boxing purses. He also dives into the real danger for the UFC in guaranteeing massive money up front if an opponent like Alex Pereira falls out and the replacement is no longer a blockbuster fight. Along the way, Chael contrasts Jones’ approach with Sean O’Malley’s, talks about the problem of renegotiating every time a fighter competes, and explains why communication can break down when everything becomes a game of telephone through managers and intermediaries. Still, Jones makes one very strong point: if the UFC truly believes he’s finished, then release him. That’s where this gets complicated. #UFC #JonJones #ChaelSonnen #AlexPereira #SeanOMalley #MMA #FightBusiness 0:00 Jon Jones is worth what his contract says 0:28 Why this is actually a Jon Jones defense 0:49 How the UFC used to pay stars extra 1:35 Why fighter pay expectations changed 2:24 How pay-per-view points became standard 3:10 What a fighter is really worth 4:12 Why Jon Jones asked for $40 million 4:36 How outside purses affect Jones’ thinking 5:06 The Dan Henderson pay-per-view example 5:53 Why guaranteed money is risky for the UFC 6:24 Alex Pereira changed Jones’ options 7:01 Why replacement opponents create a problem 8:00 The issue with constant renegotiations 9:06 Why talking about talking kills leverage 9:35 The game of telephone in fight negotiations 10:03 Sean O’Malley’s different approach 10:51 Jon Jones says release me 11:18 Why Jones may not like free agency 12:00 The UFC’s side of the Jones problem 13:03 Why Jones has to take opportunities when they come Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Francis Ngannou Sends Message to Jon Jones Over UFC Contract Dispute
articles.description: Francis Ngannou weighed in on the contract situation surrounding Jon Jones and the UFC. Ngannou spoke about fighter freedom and the challenges athletes face when negotiating with promotions. Ngannou said fighters are private contractors and should be able to walk away if they aren’t getting what they deserve: “We should be able to get what we deserve… and if we don’t get it, we should have the right to walk away and look at other options.” He also discussed how contracts can tie fighters up and lead to situations where promotions may try to lowball them during negotiations. “There is always contracts tying people up… and then when you want to set free they massage you up and lowball you.” Ngannou added that he’s happy to be free from that system now. 👀 What do you think about Francis Ngannou’s message regarding fighter contracts? #FrancisNgannou #JonJones #UFC #MMA #FighterPay #MMAnews Website: http://www.MMAWeekly.com/ Subscribe on Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/subscription_center?add_user=MMAWeeklyVideos Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/MMAWeeklycom-176775391774/ Twitter: https://twitter.com/MMAWeeklycom/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mmaweekly/

articles.title: Chael Responds To BMF Fight Criticism...
articles.description: In this episode of Ask Uncle Chael, Chael Sonnen answers fan questions about the BMF fight between Charles Oliveira and Max Holloway, the rumored UFC White House card, Bo Nickal’s matchmaking problems, and whether the UFC should ever re-sign Francis Ngannou. Chael begins by addressing the fan debate around the BMF fight. Some fans believe a “BMF fight” should mean stand-and-bang only, but Chael completely disagrees. According to him, the goal in MMA is simple: win the fight by any means necessary. Oliveira executing a smart game plan against a dangerous striker like Holloway is exactly what elite fighters are supposed to do. Chael also shares his thoughts on the speculation surrounding the UFC White House card, explaining why fans might feel slightly disappointed when big announcements are finally revealed. The guessing and speculation can sometimes be more exciting than the resolution itself — but Chael believes the card is actually stacked with the sport’s biggest stars. Later, Chael discusses Bo Nickal’s difficult matchmaking situation, explains why Francis Ngannou returning to the UFC is unlikely, and answers a fan question about passive income businesses, revealing why he believes real estate is one of the smartest long-term investments. If you enjoy hearing Chael break down the fight business and answer fan questions, this episode delivers plenty of insight. articles.description: For a limited time, our listeners get 50% off FOR LIFE, Free Shipping, AND 3 Free Gifts at Mars Men at https://www.Mengotomars.com Chael Sonnen just spoke directly with Conor McGregor — and his biggest takeaway was simple: he believed him. Chael reveals the details of a recent conversation with McGregor and explains why he came away convinced that the former UFC champion truly wants to fight again. According to Conor, he’s currently waiting on a contract from the UFC and believes his return should still be against Michael Chandler, a fight that has been discussed for years. Chael also contrasts McGregor’s situation with Jon Jones, explaining how the two stars are facing very different negotiation dynamics behind the scenes. articles.title: Chael Reacts to the UFC White House Card
articles.description: For a limited time, our listeners get 50% off FOR LIFE, Free Shipping, AND 3 Free Gifts at Mars Men at https://www.Mengotomars.com The UFC White House card is finally revealed — and Chael Sonnen says it played out almost exactly how he predicted. For weeks, rumors about the event spiraled out of control. Fans heard everything from six or seven title fights to claims that the card would happen on July 4th with no fans in attendance. Chael explains why many of those early reports never made sense and why the speculation around the event got way ahead of reality. Now that the official details are emerging, the card looks exactly like what a historic UFC event should be. Chael breaks down why the lineup works so well, highlighting the presence of some of the biggest stars in the sport — including Ilia Topuria, Sean O’Malley, and Alex Pereira. Pereira’s move to heavyweight could have massive implications for the division and may ultimately lead to a future clash with Tom Aspinall. Chael also explains why stacking an event with too many title fights would actually hurt the card instead of improving it. In UFC history, three championship fights has always been the maximum — and there’s a reason the promotion rarely goes beyond that. The result is a card built around star power, big matchups, and the right amount of championship stakes. According to Chael, this is exactly how the UFC should do it. #UFC #WhiteHouseCard #IliaTopuria #AlexPereira #SeanOMalley #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 The UFC White House card is revealed0:35 Why the early rumors were wrong1:05 The problem with too many title fights1:49 Why three titles is the limit2:09 The obsession with belts explained3:28 Why fans care about fights, not belts4:11 Pereira moving to heavyweight4:34 Ciryl Gane’s opportunity5:04 Would you co-main that fight?5:18 Why the White House card works5:26 The biggest stars on the card Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Jon Jones May Never Fight Again…
articles.description: For a limited time, our listeners get 50% off FOR LIFE, Free Shipping, AND 3 Free Gifts at Mars Men at https://www.Mengotomars.com Jon Jones may be facing a situation he can’t escape — and it might lead to retirement. Chael Sonnen breaks down the complicated position surrounding the former UFC heavyweight champion and why the options for Jones are shrinking fast. The original plan seemed simple: a blockbuster fight between Jon Jones and Tom Aspinall to unify the heavyweight titles. But everything changed when injuries, timing, and new contenders entered the picture. Chael explains how Alex Pereira potentially moving to heavyweight completely reshapes the division. If Pereira fights for an interim title against someone like Ciryl Gane, the entire path for Jon Jones disappears. Because Jones has reached such a high level of greatness, the UFC can’t simply match him with a random contender. Every fight involving Jones must feel like a massive championship-level event, which dramatically limits the available options. Chael compares the situation to Ronda Rousey’s career, where the expectations created by greatness made it difficult to continue competing without main event stakes. Add in Jones’ age, injuries, and the missed opportunity to fight on the rumored White House card, and Chael believes the evidence is pointing in one direction. articles.title: Conor Ready — But Oliveira Had Conditions #ufc
articles.description: I just spoke directly with Conor McGregor — and things almost went nuclear. Both camps. Both sides. Same message. The teams for Charles Oliveira and Max Holloway reportedly told Conor that whoever won was planning to call him out. Conor’s response? He wanted it. He welcomed it. He told both teams: if they say my name, I’m in. That’s a super fight waiting to happen. But it didn’t go that way. No callout. No faceoff. No moment. And there’s a reason. There was a caveat attached to fighting McGregor — a big one. 170 pounds. Oliveira’s side wanted the fight… but not at lightweight. Not for the belt. Different weight. Different terms. That changes everything: stakes, legacy, leverage. Super fight? Yes. Title fight? No. Negotiation fight? Absolutely. Meanwhile, Holloway stays dangerous, the division stays crowded, and the biggest star in the sport is still waiting for someone to say his name on the mic. Now Oliveira sits as reigning champion. Momentum on his side. Gold on his waist. Options everywhere. And the feeling across the sport? Something big is coming. Super fights don’t disappear — they circle. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #CharlesOliveira #MaxHolloway #FightNews #UFCRumors #Lightweight #CombatSports #Superfight

articles.title: Charles Oliveira’s Masterclass vs Holloway #ufc
articles.description: Rinse. Repeat. Dominate. Round 1 looked like Round 2. Round 2 looked like Round 3. By Round 5, the story was written. Charles Oliveira didn’t leave his skills in the locker room — he brought everything with him. World-class jiu-jitsu. Relentless pressure. Smart entries. Positional control. But first? You’ve got to get the takedown. And he did. Again and again. This wasn’t chaos. This was discipline. A decade of unfinished business led to this moment. Charles lost to Max Holloway ten years ago — and he never stopped asking for the rematch. Ten years of growth. Ten years of sharpening tools. Ten years of walking through fire to earn another shot. When it came, he was ready for all 25 minutes. Prepared. Composed. Dangerous. He hunted submissions. Threatened every scramble. Controlled every phase. And when the scorecards were read? Unanimous. All three judges. Every single round. No “round saver.” No late steal. No controversy. Just a clean, technical sweep. This is what evolution looks like. This is what persistence earns. This is championship-level maturity. Oliveira didn’t just win — he closed a 10-year chapter with authority. Legacy moment. Statement performance. Total control. #UFC #MMA #CharlesOliveira #MaxHolloway #JiuJitsu #FightBreakdown #MMANews #Lightweight #TitleRun #CombatSportsRinse. Repeat. Dominate.

articles.title: Arman vs Giorgio? Not Happening #ufc
articles.description: Let’s clear this up fast. Arman Tsarukyan vs Giorgio Petrosyan is NOT a real MMA fight waiting to happen. It’s noise. Giorgio might talk to Dana White. Fans might speculate. But the fight business doesn’t line up. Here’s why: Arman is the #1 ranked lightweight contender in the Ultimate Fighting Championship. That means one of two things: • He’s fighting for the title next • Or one more eliminator, then a title shot That’s championship traffic only. No detours. Giorgio and Arman don’t have MMA business. They don’t have jiu-jitsu business. They don’t share a division. They don’t share rankings. They don’t share a promotional lane. What they have is wrestling history. That’s it. A competitive moment. A ruleset clash. A viral incident. But wrestling drama doesn’t automatically convert into UFC matchmaking. Different sport. Different stakes. Different ladder. Arman is chasing gold — not side quests. And when you’re that close to a belt, every fight has to move you forward. Rankings matter. Timing matters. Risk management matters. This isn’t personal. It’s professional. Championship paths are narrow. Contender spots are earned. And Arman’s position is too valuable to gamble on crossover hype. If you want to talk rivalries, fine. If you want to talk headlines, sure. But if you’re talking real fights that make sense? articles.description: This is a bold move — and a smart one. Logan Paul just challenged ANY player from the NFL to beat him in a boxing match. The deal? You win — you get $1,000,000. That’s not trash talk. That’s leverage. And here’s the uncomfortable truth: he might be right. Logan isn’t just a celebrity stepping into a ring. He’s been in real fights, under real lights, with real pressure. He went eight rounds with Floyd Mayweather Jr. — one of the greatest boxers ever — and survived 30 minutes in the ring. That’s experience you can’t fake. He also handled business against Dillon Danis, showing composure, conditioning, and ring control. Meanwhile, NFL athletes are elite — but boxing is a different skill set: Footwork Distance control Timing Defense under fire Energy management over rounds World-class football shape is not fight shape. So here’s the real play: If no NFL athlete steps up, Logan wins anyway. He looks fearless. He looks confident. He controls the narrative. And he keeps building credibility in combat sports. But if someone DOES step up? Massive crossover event. Mainstream attention. Huge payday. Global spotlight. This is how modern fight promotion works — risk, spectacle, and a headline nobody can ignore. Whether you love it or hate it, it’s smart business. And right now, the ball’s in the NFL’s court. articles.title: Inside the Jones–Pereira Negotiations #ufc
articles.description: Let’s set the record straight. Jon Jones was offered Alex Pereira — and he’s in. Not “maybe.” Not “we’ll see.” He’ll fight Pereira. There’s a narrative floating around that Jones is avoiding certain matchups. That he’s picking spots. That he’s hesitant. That’s not how this went down. He received the offer. He made his position clear. And he’s mentally preparing for the fight. You don’t gear up psychologically for a war unless you plan to walk into it. But there’s another layer people ignore: negotiations. This is prizefighting. Numbers matter. Terms matter. Timing matters. Jones made his presentation. The business side is playing out the way high-level fights always do. That’s not fear — that’s leverage. The greats don’t just fight. They negotiate from value. And whether you love him or hate him, Jones has earned that position. If this matchup gets finalized, it’s massive: Champion vs champion aura Elite striker vs elite tactician Legacy stakes on both sides Pereira brings danger. Jones brings experience, IQ, and championship composure. This is the kind of fight that defines eras. So if you’re saying Jones is scared, you’re missing the reality. Fighters don’t spend decades at the top by ducking challenges — especially not ones this big. The moment the deal is signed, everything changes. And the sport gets one of its biggest possible fights. articles.title: Tom Aspinall’s Path to Two-Time Champ #ufc
articles.description: Are they sidelining Tom Aspinall… or setting him up for something bigger? There’s been real talk about title scenarios at heavyweight. Interim belts. Made-up belts. Even the idea of a one-off “White House belt.” But let’s slow down. Would it make sense to invent a new title? Probably not. Would an interim belt solve anything? Also questionable. Because when Aspinall returns, one thing is clear: He fights for the real championship. Not a novelty. Not a placeholder. The undisputed world title. And that changes the narrative completely. This isn’t about punishing Aspinall. It’s not some cold move to freeze him out. It’s opportunity. If he comes back and wins, he doesn’t just defend a belt… He becomes a two-time heavyweight world champion. That legacy hits different. Champions are remembered. Two-time champions are history-makers. Meanwhile, the division keeps moving. Big names. Big events. Big stakes. If Alex Pereira fights at heavyweight, it’s for the crown. And heavyweight title fights don’t play co-main. They headline. They define eras. So the real question isn’t whether Aspinall is being “shrimped.” It’s whether patience turns into payoff. Because timing in fight sports is everything. And the belt you win matters more than the belt you’re handed. Heavyweight history is being written. Aspinall’s chapter might just be getting started.
